description_long: This house features a steeply pitched thatched gable roof with prominent wooden beams outlining the structure. The upper story is clad in white plaster with exposed dark timber framing, characteristic of traditional half-timbered architecture. The lower portion of the house is constructed from stone and brick, providing a sturdy base and rustic appearance. Windows are small, with blue-tinted glass panes and wooden frames, arranged asymmetrically on the upper floor. The front facade includes a simple wooden door and a mix of stone and brickwork, while the rear and sides display intricate timber cross-bracing. The overall design evokes a medieval European cottage, suitable for a quaint village or historical urban setting.
description: A medieval-style half-timbered house with a thatched gable roof, white plaster walls, exposed wooden beams, and a stone-and-brick base. Small blue windows and a simple wooden door complete its rustic, storybook appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the thatched roof, a single blue-paned window, timber framing, and the stone-brick base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gable end, two blue-paned windows, and symmetrical timber framing.
description_view_2: Rear view with the thatched roof, a single window, cross-braced timber, and the main entry door at the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying extensive timber cross-bracing and no visible windows.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door at ground level, no porch or steps, framed by stone and brickwork
